name: standards-extraction
description: Extract coding standards and conventions from CONTRIBUTING.md, .editorconfig, linter configs. Use for onboarding and ensuring consistent contributions.
capabilities: Parse CONTRIBUTING.md for commit formats and branch naming rules
Extract formatting rules from .editorconfig and Prettier configs
Read ESLint, Ruff, and other linter configuration settings
Check for git hooks in .husky and pre-commit configs
Compile all standards into structured JSON output
author: Consiliency
securitySummary: Pure documentation skill with zero executable code. Static findings are false positives triggered by documentation examples containing code snippets and metadata hashes. All 57 flagged patterns are benign documentation content.
valueStatement: New contributors often guess coding conventions instead of following established project standards. This skill parses CONTRIBUTING.md, linter configs, and editor settings to return a complete standards summary for consistent contributions.
userTitle: Extract coding standards from project configs
limitations: Only reads standard config file formats (JSON, YAML, TOML, INI)
Does not execute linting or formatting tools
Requires AI agent to have file system access to target project
Cannot validate that code actually follows extracted rules

useCases.title: Onboard to codebase
useCases.description: Get complete list of coding conventions before writing first line of code

useCases.title: Audit project standards
useCases.description: Verify all project standards are documented and accessible in one place

useCases.title: Follow project conventions
useCases.description: Ensure AI-generated code matches established project style guidelines

promptTemplates.title: Basic extraction
promptTemplates.prompt: Use the standards-extraction skill to parse all config files in this project and return the coding standards summary.

promptTemplates.title: Formatting focus
promptTemplates.prompt: Extract only the formatting rules (indentation, line length, quotes, semicolons) from this project's .editorconfig and Prettier config.

promptTemplates.title: Linting rules
promptTemplates.prompt: Parse the ESLint and linter configurations in this project and list the key enabled rules and extended configs.

promptTemplates.title: Full contribution guide
promptTemplates.prompt: Extract the complete contribution guidelines including commit message format, branch naming, PR requirements, and testing expectations.

bestPractices: Run extraction on the target project root directory for complete results
Combine with code review skills to validate contributions match standards
Use extracted rules as input for automated linting in CI pipelines
antiPatterns: Skipping CONTRIBUTING.md because it might be outdated
Assuming generic coding standards without checking project configs
Reporting formatting rules that contradict actual config files

faq.question: Which config files does this skill support?
faq.answer: Supports ESLint, Prettier, Ruff, EditorConfig, .husky, pre-commit, pyproject.toml, and common markdown contribution guides.

faq.question: Does this skill execute any code on my system?
faq.answer: No. This is a prompt-based skill that provides instructions. It does not run executables or access files directly.

faq.question: Can I use this with Claude Code CLI?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill works with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code. File access depends on the tool you use.

faq.question: What output formats are available?
faq.answer: Supports JSON, markdown, and toon formats. Use the OUTPUT_FORMAT variable to specify your preference.

faq.question: Does this send data to external servers?
faq.answer: No. This skill only provides instructions to the AI. No network calls are made by the skill itself.

faq.question: How is this different from running eslint --print-config?
faq.answer: This skill reads config files and contribution guides to explain standards. It does not run the actual tools or validate code.
